strengths: Real-world contexts are clearly specified for two of the three objects (Einstein letter and violin score), grounding the exhibition in specific historical settings.
Links between each object and the IA prompt (the role of imagination in extending knowledge) are explicit and well-articulated across science, technology and the arts.
Justifications move beyond mere description to consider TOK issuesâe.g., balancing imaginative insight with empirical constraints, and the paradigm-shifting role of Galileoâs telescope.
Inclusion of a personal artefact demonstrates the interplay between personal and shared knowledge, enriching the TOK focus on how individual interpretation contributes to artistic knowledge.ï»¿
weaknesses: Provenance of Galileoâs telescope is imprecise and accompanied only by a reproduction image, weakening the real-world grounding.
The selected IA prompt is never explicitly named, leaving the exhibitionâs coherence under-anchored.
Frequent factual and citation inconsistencies (e.g. historical dates, incomplete sentences, reference formatting errors) blur the precision expected at the top of the band.
Several arguments are left incomplete or under-developed (truncated paragraphs, unsupported claims about performance practice, generic conclusions), limiting the depth of TOK analysis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The strand assessment places the work firmly at the lower end of band 7â8: it demonstrates clear object selection, context and TOK-focused justification, yet is weakened by imprecision in provenance, factual and citation errors, and uneven development of critical points. Awarding 7 marks reflects this strong but not flawless performance, in line with the official examinerâs total and the rubricâs descriptors for 7â8.

annotations.comment: The text inaccurately suggests Galileo âcreatedâ the telescope in 1609 but later states he did not invent it; correct this historical inconsistency to maintain factual precision.
The timeline is inconsistent: note is from 2014 but you reference playing at age seven; clarify dates and age to maintain factual accuracy.
The student has not explicitly identified which of the prescribed IA prompts is being addressed; stating the selected prompt will anchor the analysis and strengthen the exhibitionâs coherence.
The provenance of Galileoâs telescope is vague (âa museum in Italyâ); specify the museum and collection details to strengthen the objectâs real-world grounding.
The final summary statement is perfunctory; instead of restating the prompt, reframe to address implications for knowledge frameworks in TOK.
The connection between imagining historic performance and acquiring new knowledge in the arts is well articulated, demonstrating a clear link to the IA prompt.
The critique of authenticity introduces a valuable limitation but lacks examples; provide specific stylistic conventions that imagination might misinterpret for a richer analysis.
The exhibitionâs title succinctly frames the investigation into imagination as a knowledge-producing tool, providing a clear entry point that aligns well with the TOK focus.
The justification paragraph is truncated mid-sentence, leaving the argument incomplete; ensure closure of your reasoning to deliver a full, persuasive justification.
This assertion about FornÃ¤sâs contribution is purely descriptive and lacks direct connection to the prompt; extend the analysis to show how imagination generated FornÃ¤sâs alternative model.
Real-world context is well established in the captionâdate, recipient and museum catalogue number are clearly documented, grounding the object in a specific historical setting.
The description recounts pedagogical applications but fails to explicitly connect these uses back to the role of imagination in knowledge creation; integrate TOK terminology to clarify this link.

The historical example of rain-origin beliefs is interesting but introduced without clear in-text citation or explicit link to imaginationâs function; provide a precise reference and reinforce the TOK connection.
The conclusion here is generic; illustrate how simplified imaginative models have concretely led to scientific breakthroughs to deepen the TOK insight.
Using an image of a modern reproduction weakens authenticity; where possible, include an image of the original artefact or clarify its status as a reproduction.
The provenance description of your violin notes is personal but lacks broader context; specify how these pages fit into a larger collection or tradition to bolster shared knowledge dimensions.
The discussion does articulate how Galileoâs imaginative use of the telescope challenged prevailing paradigms, effectively linking imagination to paradigm shift in scientific knowledge.
The sentence ends abruptly (âHe didnâtâ); complete this thought to fully express how empirical validation balanced against imaginative conjecture.
This conclusion restates earlier points rather than offering new insight; develop a distinct TOK perspective on how imagination and evidence interplay distinctly for this object.
The claim that imagination can produce oversimplified knowledge is valid but under-developed; illustrate with a concrete example of how that simplification was later challenged by evidence.
Including a personal artefact demonstrates the interplay between personal and shared knowledge, highlighting how individual interpretation contributes to the evolution of artistic knowledge.
This narrative description would benefit from explicit TOK language: discuss how imagination mediates between prescribed Baroque conventions and personal expression.
The justification remains superficial; integrate a cited example of Baroque performance practice to substantiate your claim about imaginative interpretation.
The concluding reflection reiterates earlier ideas without advancing a new TOK implication; consider posing an explicit knowledge question to deepen the exhibitionâs critical edge.
Formatting of the Koestler reference is inconsistent with other entries; adopt a uniform citation style (author, date, title, source) to enhance academic rigour.
The Nobel Prize Museum entry misnames the recipient (âEvery FornÃ¤sâ); correct factual details in references to ensure accuracy.
The Rosenstein reference appears in the text but lacks consistent date formatting; verify that all in-text citations correspond exactly to reference list entries.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
